scores.anchor_matched: anchor at score 5, capped by the runbook count of >=3 btn-primary matches on sale.html and duplicate home-page primary create links

evidence.note: Hero has one primary plus two secondary links, but the rankings section repeats '+ Create new futarchy' as a second btn-primary on the same page.
Buy column is a compact action surface with quick amount buttons, adjacent amount input, cost row, and one buy primary.
Sell column mirrors the Buy column, but the hidden confirmation card adds a third btn-primary in sale.html, triggering the rubric runbook D1 cap at 5.

scores.anchor_matched: between anchors 3 and 5, capped at 5 by missing EIP-6963 provider discovery

evidence.note: Connect uses bare window.ethereum, eth_requestAccounts, wallet_switchEthereumChain, and then displays only a truncated address instead of provider name plus address.
Global chainChanged and accountsChanged handlers reset activeSigner and connectedWallet and dispatch fao:walletChanged.
Proposals page keeps a separate bare-window.ethereum connect flow, switches chain inline, and updates the button without provider identity.
Bond flow duplicates signer acquisition with window.ethereum and has local account/chain reset listeners, but still no EIP-6963 wallet picker.

scores.dimension: D3 — Pre-confirm / pending / success / error affordances
scores.anchor_matched: between anchors 3 and 5; sale trade flows have review cards, but several chain writes still jump straight to wallet and key status regions lack aria-live

evidence.note: Sale page has an in-page pre-confirm card, but the dynamic sale-buy-status node has no aria-live attribute.
Buy preview decodes action, amount, payment, sale address, and recipient before the wallet-confirm button is used.
Buy execution distinguishes wallet approval and mining, but the mining state does not expose an Etherscan tx-hash link.
Create-futarchy submits Part1 and Part2 transactions after connect with tx links, but there is no canonical pre-confirm review card before the wallet popup.
Proposal submission sends createProposal directly after form validation; status links the tx hash, but the flow lacks an in-page decoded review.
YES-bond flow prompts for amount and then proceeds through WETH checks, approval, and placeYesBond without a shared pre-confirm review card.

scores.anchor_matched: between anchors 5 and 7; data-dense trade surfaces, but tabular numerals and prose discipline are not universal

evidence.note: First sale viewport exposes token identity, phase badge, contract link, and sale price before the trade grid.
Sale stats and raw contract metadata are collapsed behind details rather than inline in the primary trade surface.
Home stack section presents actionable numeric rows such as latest block, proposals created, timeout, TWAP window, fee tier, tests, and simulation hours.
Sale hero price uses tabular numerals.
Ranking numeric cells use the monospace font and right alignment, but do not explicitly set font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums.
Create page leads with a long implementation-paraphrase paragraph, which adds prose without immediate decision-time numbers.

scores.anchor_matched: anchor at score 5 with partial progress toward 7; responsive and focus/reduced-motion basics exist, but labels, live regions, and small tap targets remain inconsistent

evidence.note: Legacy sale-buy grid collapses to one column at <=760px and sale hero stacks on mobile.
Current trade-grid Buy/Sell columns collapse to one column at <=760px.
Create-futarchy form fields are wrapped in explicit labels.
Proposal name and description inputs are placeholder-only and create-status has no aria-live attribute.
Quick amount buttons use 4px vertical padding and no min-height, so they likely miss the 44px action-surface target size.
Bond action buttons use 6px vertical padding with 12px text and no min-height, leaving several controls below 44px.
Topbar status is created with role=status and aria-live=polite, but this is not applied to sale/create/proposal/bond status nodes.
Reduced-motion override and focus-visible outlines are present.

scores.anchor_matched: anchor at score 3-5; one stylesheet and token block exist, but D6 is capped at 5 by >8 distinct font-size values and pulled lower by raw color/gradient/shadow drift

evidence.note: Central :root defines core color and typography variables for the dark theme.
The stylesheet already uses many distinct type sizes in the base, nav, section, and hero rules; the runbook probe found 15 unique font-size declarations, capping D6 at 5.
Phase badge colors introduce multiple raw accent-style colors beyond one accent plus green/red semantics.
Ranking Buy action uses raw green hex values instead of bound CSS variables.
Sale symbol and progress fill use decorative gradients in the trade surface, conflicting with the no-chartjunk/minimalism anchor.
Home stats inject inline style color hex literals, an AP6 design-token override.
Live-dot and stale states add glow shadows; additional dropdown/modal shadows appear elsewhere, exceeding the <=2 shadow-level target.
The FAO modal adds a 0 12px 48px shadow level separate from glow and menu shadows.

evaluator_notes: No prior audit/evaluations/topic-1-evals.jsonl entry existed, so prior-score regressions and improvements are empty. I did not fetch the live URL per the rubric. Current repo state removes native alert/confirm/prompt calls and adds reduced-motion/topbar aria-live support, but hard caps remain from missing EIP-6963, missing pre-confirm review on create/proposal/bond writes, missing aria-live on most status nodes, and D6 runbook/style-token violations.

scores.anchor_matched: anchor at score 5; capped at 5 by the rubric runbook because sale.html has >=3 btn-primary matches including the hidden confirm primary, and the home page repeats the Create primary CTA

evidence.note: Hero has one primary plus two secondary links, but the rankings header repeats '+ Create new futarchy' as another btn-primary on the same page.
Buy column has quick amounts next to the amount input and one visible primary action for buying via sale.
Sell column mirrors Buy with one visible primary for Ragequit, but the hidden confirmation card adds a third btn-primary in sale.html, triggering the D1 cap.

Proposal creation keeps a separate bare-window.ethereum connect path, switches chain inline, and disables the button after showing only the address.

evaluator_notes: The rubric scope explicitly limits Topic 1 scoring to site-testnet plus the rubric/research docs, so the new audit specs, invariants tests, CI workflows, NatSpec tags, and wiki files were treated as out-of-scope for this Web3 UX score. scores.dimension: D3 — Pre-confirm / pending / success / error affordances
scores.anchor_matched: low score-5 band; browser-native confirm/prompt were replaced with in-page modal helpers and sale writes have decoded review cards, but multiple chain writes still skip a canonical pre-confirm card and sale tx states lack explorer links

evidence.note: Sale page provides a reusable pre-confirm card for trade flows, but sale-buy-status itself is still not declared aria-live.
Buy preview decodes action, amount, payment, sale address, and recipient before the wallet-confirm button.
Uniswap buy distinguishes wallet approval from mining, but after tx submission the status does not expose an Etherscan tx-hash link.
Sale buy distinguishes wallet approval and mining, but sets Mining without a tx link and then closeConfirmCard clears the success status.
Create-futarchy still sends Part1 and Part2 transactions without an in-page decoded review before the first wallet popup, though submitted txs are linked.
Proposal submission goes directly from validation to createProposal; it links the tx hash but lacks an in-page review card.
Bond wrap and amount entry now use faoConfirm/faoPrompt in-page helpers, then submit approval/placeYesBond with tx-hash status links.

improvement_vs_prior.cause: Executable browser-native confirm/prompt calls were removed from bond inputs and replaced with shared in-page modal helpers; sale review-card coverage is unchanged.

scores.anchor_matched: between anchors 5 and 7; native modals, reduced-motion, and focus-ring gaps improved, but status live regions, proposal labels, no-JS fallback, and small secondary tap targets still block 7

evidence.note: Shared modal host uses role=dialog, aria-modal, aria-labelledby, Escape handling, backdrop cancel, and initial focus.
Topbar status is role=status with aria-live=polite for connect failures.
Reduced-motion override and global focus-visible outlines are present.
Sale hero and legacy sale-buy grid stack at <=760px.
Current Buy/Sell trade grid collapses to one column at <=760px.
Create-futarchy form controls have explicit labels and data-testid hooks, but the status node lacks aria-live.
Proposal name/description controls remain placeholder-only and create-status lacks aria-live.
Quick amount buttons still use 4px vertical padding and no min-height, below the 44px target for primary-adjacent controls.
Bond action buttons still use 6px vertical padding with 12px text and no min-height.

improvement_vs_prior.cause: The executable alert/confirm/prompt calls are gone, shared modal helpers add dialog semantics/focus handling, reduced-motion handling exists, and focus-visible outlines are present.

scores.anchor_matched: below anchor 5 and capped at 5 by >8 distinct font-size values; tokens.css is a real design-token lift, but styles.css still overrides tokens and carries raw colors, gradients, extra type sizes, and shadow drift

evidence.note: New documented design-token file states color, spacing, radius, and type-scale intent for Topic 1 D6.
tokens.css centralizes palette, spacing, radius, type scale, shadow, focus, motion, and button tokens.
styles.css still declares a second :root block after tokens.css is loaded, overriding core token values rather than consuming the token file as the single source.
Ranking and phase styles still include raw semantic/accent hex and rgba values outside token variables.
Ranking Buy action uses raw green hex values instead of var(--accent)/var(--accent-hover).
Sale symbol and progress fill use decorative gradients in the trade surface.
Home stats still inject inline color hex literals, triggering the AP6 design-token override penalty.
Modal card shadow adds another visible shadow level beyond live-dot glow/dropdown styles.

improvement_vs_prior.cause: tokens.css now documents and centralizes intended design tokens, but the runbook font-size cap and remaining token drift keep D6 below 5.

improvements.dimension: D3 — Pre-confirm / pending / success / error affordances
improvements.cause: Bond confirm/prompt inputs moved to shared in-page modal helpers; no executable browser-native modal calls remain.
Shared modal accessibility, aria-live topbar status, reduced-motion handling, and focus-visible outlines landed.
tokens.css provides a documented token system, though styles.css still overrides and bypasses it in many places.

evaluator_notes: Scoped strictly to Topic 1 per rubric lines 3-8 and 24-34: Solidity invariant tests, audit/specs, precondition docs, foundry.toml CI profile, deployments.json, static/symbolic workflows, and journey scaffolds are useful for other topics but do not count for this Web3 UX score. I did not fetch the live site. Lower-anchor observations were not averaged away: D1 remains capped by btn-primary counts, D2 remains capped by missing EIP-6963, D3 still has direct create/proposal/resolve/bond writes without a shared review card and sale tx statuses without tx links, D5 still lacks aria-live on primary status nodes/no-JS fallback, and D6 remains capped by the type-ramp runbook probe.

styles.css declares the contract to consume tokens.css and not re-declare :root.

evaluator_notes: HEAD verified as 030d258e6d7909b3e724f1a7cc5cd7f4f711178c. Diff from prior evaluated commit 535ec42a246382bc3a5b9781c8bf4188425a5799 changes site-testnet/shared.js, site-testnet/create.js, and 28 site-testnet/abis/*.json files inside Topic 1 scope. shared.js now has localStorage fork-RPC selection and a cached ABI loader; create.js uses loadFaoAbi('FutarchyRegistry') instead of an inline ABI. These are front-end architecture improvements but do not satisfy the rubric's blocking behavioral anchors for EIP-6963, provider identity, shared pre-confirm review, status aria-live coverage, tap-target sizing, or D6 typography/style caps. The other named lifts are out of Topic 1 scoring under rubric lines 3-8 and 24-34: site-ops, audit/dashboard, tests, CI, scripts, deployment schema, Etherscan verification, Playwright fork project, and CAO worker profiles are not the site-testnet UX surface being scored. Runbook probes at this commit show no executable alert/confirm/prompt calls, no EIP-6963 provider discovery, one btn-primary match per checked page, six tabular-numeral rules, 16 unique hardcoded styles.css hex colors, and 15 unique font-size declarations. All scores are unchanged versus the prior Topic 1 entry, so no regression or improvement threshold fired. No live URL was fetched.
